title: Tuple Indexing and Access
description: Master accessing elements within tuples using positive and negative indexing. Write functions to retrieve the first element, last element, all middle elements (excluding first and last), and a fully reversed copy of any given tuple. These patterns are essential for tuple data extraction.
hints: Use index 0 for the first element and index -1 for the last element of a tuple.
Slicing with t[1:-1] gives you everything except the first and last elements.
The slice t[::-1] reverses any sequence, including tuples.

testCases.description: middle_elements returns everything except first and last
middle_elements returns empty tuple when only two elements
reverse_tuple works on a single-element tuple
